description: Bouncing ball with realistic gravity, bounce dampening, and a trail effect. The ball drops from the top of the viewport, bounces off the bottom with decreasing bounce height each time, and leaves a fading trail of its previous positions. The ball must remain within the viewport at all times.
requirements: Ball starts at top-center of viewport and falls under simulated gravity (acceleration ~9.8 units/s^2 scaled to pixels)
Each bounce reduces velocity by 30% (coefficient of restitution 0.7)
Ball leaves a trail of at least 20 semi-transparent copies that fade from 0.5 to 0 opacity over 500ms
Ball must never escape the viewport boundaries (top, bottom, left, right)
Animation runs for at least 5 seconds showing multiple bounces
Ball is a filled circle with radius 15px and a subtle radial gradient
Background is dark (#1a1a2e) to make the trail visible
expected_issues: Ball escapes below the viewport floor due to discrete time steps overshooting the boundary
Trail effect renders as solid line instead of fading dots
Bounce dampening not applied correctly causing infinite bouncing at full height
Gravity accumulation error causing ball to accelerate unrealistically
Ball sticks to the floor instead of coming to rest gracefully

description: Pendulum simulation with a swinging bob, taut string visualization, and a shadow projected below the bob on a ground plane. The pendulum swings back and forth with realistic deceleration due to simulated air resistance.
requirements: Pivot point is at top-center of the viewport
String length is 250px, rendered as a 2px line from pivot to bob
Bob is a filled circle of radius 20px with a gradient fill
Pendulum starts at 45 degrees from vertical and swings with decreasing amplitude
Shadow is an ellipse on a ground line at the bottom, whose horizontal position tracks the bob and whose size varies with the bob's height (larger when bob is higher)
String must remain taut at all times (constant length between pivot and bob)
Animation uses accurate pendulum physics: angular acceleration = -(g/L)*sin(theta) with a small damping term
Period of oscillation should be approximately 1 second
expected_issues: String stretches or detaches from the bob during swing
Pendulum moves linearly instead of along an arc
Shadow does not track the bob's horizontal position
Small-angle approximation used incorrectly causing unrealistic motion at large angles
Damping is too aggressive or absent, causing immediate stop or perpetual motion

description: Particle explosion effect: clicking anywhere on the page triggers an explosion of 50+ particles that fly outward radially from the click point with randomized velocities, decelerate due to friction, shrink, and fade out over 1.5 seconds.
requirements: Each click spawns at least 50 particles at the click coordinates
Particles are small circles (3-8px radius, randomized) with random colors from a warm palette (reds, oranges, yellows)
Particles fly outward in all 360 degrees with randomized initial speeds between 100-400 px/s
Particles decelerate due to friction (velocity multiplied by 0.97 each frame)
Particles fade from opacity 1.0 to 0.0 over their 1.5-second lifetime
Particles shrink to 0 radius by end of lifetime
Multiple explosions can coexist on screen simultaneously
Uses canvas element for rendering, running at 60fps via requestAnimationFrame
Finished particles are removed from memory to prevent leaks
expected_issues: Particles all move in the same direction instead of radially outward
Particles cluster together instead of spreading uniformly in all directions
Fade and shrink are not synchronized with particle lifetime
Memory leak from particles never being cleaned up, causing slowdown over time
Particles spawn at (0,0) instead of click coordinates
All particles have same color instead of randomized palette

description: Water ripple effect: clicking the canvas creates concentric circles that expand outward from the click point with decreasing opacity and increasing radius. Multiple ripple sources should coexist and the circles should render with a water-like blue tint.
requirements: Each click creates a ripple source that emits 5 concentric expanding rings
Rings expand outward at 80px/s with 60px spacing between consecutive rings
Each ring starts at opacity 0.8 and fades to 0 by the time it reaches 300px radius
Rings are rendered as circle outlines (not filled) with 2px stroke width
Ring color transitions from light blue (#4fc3f7) at center to transparent at max radius
Multiple ripple sources can be active simultaneously without visual conflicts
Ripple sources are cleaned up after all their rings have fully faded
Canvas background is a dark blue gradient (#0d1b2a to #1b263b)
Animation runs at 60fps using requestAnimationFrame
expected_issues: Concentric circles overlap with incorrect compositing causing bright spots
Rings disappear too quickly before reaching full expansion
New ripple sources erase or interfere with existing ones
Ring spacing is uniform in time instead of space, causing bunching
Opacity fade is linear when it should follow the ring's radial distance
Canvas not cleared between frames causing ghosting

description: Elastic collision simulation: two balls of different masses and colors move toward each other from opposite sides of the screen, collide in the center, and bounce off each other with conservation of momentum and kinetic energy. After collision, they continue to the edges and bounce off the walls.
requirements: Ball A: radius 25px, mass 2 units, red (#e74c3c), starts at left edge moving right at 150px/s
Ball B: radius 18px, mass 1 unit, blue (#3498db), starts at right edge moving left at 200px/s
Collision detection based on distance between centers being less than sum of radii
Post-collision velocities computed using elastic collision formulas preserving momentum and kinetic energy
Balls bounce off the left and right walls of the viewport
Display velocity vectors as arrows extending from each ball's center in the direction of motion
Show a real-time display of total system momentum and total kinetic energy (should remain constant)
Animation runs for 10 seconds with continuous wall bouncing
Trail effect: each ball leaves a fading trail of its last 30 positions
expected_issues: Balls pass through each other instead of colliding
Balls stick together after collision and move as one
Momentum or energy not conserved — displayed values drift over time
Collision detected multiple times in consecutive frames causing jitter
Velocity vectors point in wrong direction after collision
Balls escape through walls due to discrete time step overshooting

description: 3D card flip animation: a card with distinct front and back faces rotates 180 degrees around its vertical axis when clicked, revealing the back side. Clicking again flips it back. The flip should have realistic 3D perspective with proper backface handling.
requirements: Card is 300px wide by 400px tall, centered on screen
Front face: white background with a large spade suit symbol and 'A' in corners (like an Ace of Spades)
Back face: a repeating diagonal pattern in red and blue on a white background
Flip animation takes 600ms with ease-in-out timing
3D perspective applied to parent container (perspective: 1000px)
Backface of each side must be hidden during the flip (backface-visibility: hidden)
During the flip, the card should appear to have thickness (subtle edge shadow)
A slight scale increase to 1.05 at the midpoint of the flip (3D pop effect)
Card casts a dynamic shadow that moves during the flip
Clicking during an ongoing flip queues the next flip to start after current one finishes
expected_issues: Both faces visible simultaneously during the flip (backface-visibility not set)
Content on the back face appears mirrored/reversed
3D perspective is missing causing flat 2D scaling instead of rotation
Card disappears at the 90-degree midpoint where it is edge-on
Clicking during animation causes glitched state with partial rotation
Shadow does not update during animation

description: Loading spinner composed of 8 dots arranged in a perfect circle. The dots light up sequentially with a trailing glow that fades across the 3 dots behind the active one, creating a smooth chasing effect.
requirements: 8 dots arranged in a circle of radius 40px, centered in the viewport
Each dot is 12px diameter
Dots are gray (#444) by default and cyan (#00e5ff) when active
Active dot rotates clockwise, completing one full revolution in 1 second
The 3 dots trailing behind the active one show decreasing brightness (opacity 0.7, 0.4, 0.15)
Each dot has a glow effect (box-shadow) when active that matches its opacity level
The transition between states is smooth (not instant snap)
Spinner is centered vertically and horizontally in viewport
Below the spinner, text reads 'Loading...' with an animated ellipsis (cycling: '.', '..', '...')
expected_issues: Dots arranged in a line or grid instead of a circle
Trailing glow applies to wrong dots (leading instead of trailing)
All dots light up simultaneously instead of sequentially
Timing is uneven causing the chase to stutter or skip dots
Glow effect missing or not scaling with opacity
Dots are not evenly spaced around the circle

description: Accordion menu with 5 collapsible sections. Clicking a section header expands its content with a smooth height animation while simultaneously collapsing any currently open section. Only one section can be open at a time.
requirements: 5 sections with headers: 'About Us', 'Services', 'Portfolio', 'Team', 'Contact'
Each section's content area contains 3-4 lines of placeholder text
Expand/collapse animates the height over 400ms with ease-in-out timing
An arrow icon on each header rotates 180 degrees when its section is open
Only one section is open at a time — opening one closes any other open section
The first section starts open by default
Header background darkens on hover
Open section header has a distinct left border accent (4px solid #2196F3)
Content area has padding that is included in the height animation (no jump at end)
Clicking an already-open section closes it (all sections closed is a valid state)
expected_issues: Multiple sections can be open simultaneously
Height animation shows a jump at the start or end (content padding not accounted for)
Content overflows its container during the collapsing animation
Arrow rotation is not synchronized with expand/collapse
Rapid clicking causes sections to get stuck in intermediate states
Height is hardcoded rather than calculated from actual content

description: Animated progress bar that fills from 0% to 100% over exactly 3 seconds. The bar has a gradient fill, a percentage label that updates in real-time, and a confetti celebration animation when it reaches 100%.
requirements: Progress bar container is 500px wide, 30px tall, centered horizontally near the top third of the page
Bar fill uses a left-to-right gradient from #4CAF50 to #8BC34A with a subtle animated shimmer effect
Percentage text is centered on the bar and reads '0%' to '100%', updating every frame
Text color switches from dark to white when the bar passes 50% fill (for contrast)
Bar fill takes exactly 3000ms using linear timing
At 100%, a confetti burst of 80+ small colored rectangles erupts from the bar, falling with gravity and rotation
Confetti pieces are randomized in color (at least 6 distinct colors), size (4-10px), rotation speed, and horizontal spread
Confetti falls for 3 seconds then fades out
A checkmark icon and 'Complete!' text appear above the bar at 100%
A 'Restart' button appears after completion that resets the entire animation
expected_issues: Percentage text does not sync with actual bar width
Bar overshoots 100% width or percentage shows values above 100
Confetti triggers before bar reaches 100% or does not trigger at all
Shimmer effect causes visual artifacts or flickers
Text color switch happens at wrong threshold or not at all
Restart button does not fully reset state causing doubled confetti on second run

description: Shape morphing animation: a shape smoothly transitions from a circle to a square to a triangle and back to a circle, cycling continuously. Each morph takes 2 seconds (6-second total cycle).
requirements: Shape is rendered on a canvas element, centered in the viewport
Shape fill is a gradient that rotates slowly (independent of morphing)
Circle: radius 80px, defined by 64 evenly spaced points
Square: 160x160px, defined by 4 corners with points distributed along edges (64 total points to match circle)
Triangle: equilateral with 160px side length, defined by 3 corners with points along edges (64 total)
Morphing interpolates each of the 64 points from source shape to target shape positions
Interpolation uses ease-in-out timing (not linear) for organic feel
Shape has a 3px stroke outline in a darker shade of the fill color
A label below shows the current transition (e.g., 'Circle -> Square') and a progress percentage
Animation loops infinitely
expected_issues: Vertices jump abruptly instead of interpolating smoothly between shapes
Point distribution along shape edges is uneven causing pinching during morph
Triangle morph collapses because point correspondence is wrong (points not mapped logically between shapes)
Shape flickers or disappears at transition boundaries between morph stages
Canvas not cleared properly causing overlapping shapes from previous frames
Gradient does not rotate or causes visual artifacts

description: Solar system animation: a sun at the center with 4 planets orbiting at different distances and speeds. Each planet has one moon that orbits the planet. Orbits are shown as faint dotted circles.
requirements: Sun: 40px radius, yellow (#FDB813) with a radial glow effect, centered in viewport
Planet 1 (Mercury): 8px radius, gray (#B0B0B0), orbit radius 80px, orbital period 3s
Planet 2 (Earth): 12px radius, blue (#4A90D9), orbit radius 140px, orbital period 5s
Planet 3 (Mars): 10px radius, red (#C1440E), orbit radius 200px, orbital period 7s
Planet 4 (Jupiter): 18px radius, orange (#C88B3A), orbit radius 280px, orbital period 11s
Each planet has a moon: 4px radius, white, orbiting its planet at 20px distance with a 1s orbital period
Orbit paths rendered as faint dotted circles (opacity 0.2)
Moons must track their parent planet's position as the planet orbits the sun
All orbits are circular and move counterclockwise
Planets cast small shadows (dark circle offset slightly from planet) to suggest a light source from the sun
Canvas renders at 60fps
expected_issues: Moons orbit around the sun instead of their parent planet
Moons lose tracking when the parent planet moves, drifting away
All planets orbit at the same speed despite different period specifications
Orbit paths are solid lines instead of dotted circles
Planet sizes are too similar to distinguish
Z-ordering issues where inner planets render on top of outer planet orbits

description: Typing effect with a blinking cursor. Three different messages are displayed one after another: each message is typed out character by character, pauses, then is deleted character by character before the next message begins. The cycle repeats.
requirements: Messages: 'Hello, I am a creative developer.', 'I build beautiful web experiences.', 'Let us create something amazing together.'
Typing speed: 70ms per character
Deletion speed: 40ms per character
Pause of 1500ms after fully typing a message before deletion begins
Pause of 500ms after fully deleting before typing next message
Cursor is a solid vertical bar '|' that blinks (visible 530ms, hidden 530ms) when paused, and stays visible while typing or deleting
Font: 32px monospace, white text on dark background (#1e1e1e)
Text container is centered horizontally and vertically
Text must not wrap mid-animation — container is wide enough for the longest message
The entire sequence (all 3 messages) cycles infinitely
expected_issues: Cursor disappears during typing or deletion phases
Cursor blinks during active typing when it should be solid
Text wraps to the next line causing layout jumps during typing
Deletion removes wrong characters or deletes in wrong direction
Messages don't cycle — stops after the third message
Timing between type and delete phases is missing, causing jarring transitions

description: Animated bar chart race: 5 named data bars compete for rank over 5 time periods. Every 2 seconds, the bars smoothly animate to new heights and reorder their vertical positions based on the new ranking. Labels and values stay attached to their bars throughout.
requirements: 5 bars with labels: 'Alpha' (red), 'Beta' (blue), 'Gamma' (green), 'Delta' (orange), 'Epsilon' (purple)
Bars are horizontal, extending from the left side
Bar widths represent values, ranging from 50 to 500 pixels
Predefined data for 5 time periods with changing values that cause rank reordering
Every 2 seconds, bars smoothly transition to new widths AND vertically reorder to maintain sorted rank (longest bar on top)
Width and position transitions take 800ms with ease-in-out timing
Each bar has its label on the left and its numeric value displayed at the right end of the bar
A time period indicator shows the current period (e.g., 'Year 2021', 'Year 2022', etc.)
Bars maintain consistent height (40px) and spacing (10px gap) during reordering
After the 5th period, the animation loops back to period 1
expected_issues: Labels detach from their bars during vertical reordering
Bars overlap or clip through each other during the reorder transition
Values shown on bars don't update or lag behind the width change
Rank reordering happens instantly instead of animating smoothly
Bar widths go negative or exceed container bounds
Time period label does not update in sync with bar transitions

description: Stick figure walking up a staircase of 6 steps. The figure moves from the bottom-left to the top-right, stepping up onto each step with a simple walking leg animation. The body rises and shifts right in a stair-step pattern.
requirements: Staircase has 6 steps, each 80px wide and 50px tall, rendered in gray (#888) with darker edges
Stick figure: circle head (15px radius), line body, two line arms, two line legs, total height ~60px
Figure starts at the bottom of the first step and walks up to the top of the last step
Walking animation: legs alternate in a simple two-frame walk cycle (front leg forward, back leg back, then swap)
Leg cycle rate: 4 steps per second (each leg position held for 250ms)
Figure moves horizontally across each step over 1 second, then steps up to the next level over 0.5 seconds
During the step-up, the leading leg reaches up to the next step while the body rises
Figure's feet must align with the step surfaces at all times (no floating or sinking)
Arms swing opposite to legs (left arm forward when right leg forward)
Total animation duration: approximately 9 seconds (6 steps with traversal and climbing)
expected_issues: Stick figure floats above or sinks below the step surfaces
Legs do not alternate properly — both move in the same direction
Figure teleports between steps instead of smoothly climbing
Arms and legs move independently from body position
Figure's horizontal position does not align with step width
Step-up transition has the figure clip through the step edge

description: Day-night cycle animation: the sky transitions through a full day cycle (day, sunset, night, sunrise) over 10 seconds. A sun and moon object move in arcs across the sky. Stars appear during the night phase and fade during day.
requirements: Sky fills the entire viewport as a vertical gradient background
Day phase (0-2s): sky gradient from #87CEEB (top) to #E0F7FA (bottom), sun is a yellow (#FFD700) circle with rays
Sunset phase (2-4s): sky transitions to gradient from #FF6B35 (top) to #FFC107 (bottom), sun descends to horizon
Night phase (4-7s): sky gradient from #0D1B2A (top) to #1B263B (bottom), moon rises as a crescent (white circle with dark circle overlay), 50+ stars twinkle
Sunrise phase (7-9s): sky transitions from night to gradient from #FF8A65 (top) to #FFCC80 (bottom), moon descends, sun rises
Return to day (9-10s): sky returns to day gradient, sun resumes position
Sun/moon follow a smooth arc path (parabolic or sinusoidal) from one horizon to the other
Stars are small white dots (2-4px) at random positions that fade in during sunset and fade out during sunrise
At least 5 stars twinkle (oscillate opacity between 0.3 and 1.0 at random rates)
A ground silhouette (dark hills or cityscape) remains at the bottom throughout
Animation loops seamlessly
expected_issues: Sky color transitions jump abruptly instead of smoothly interpolating
Sun and moon teleport between positions instead of following an arc
Stars are visible during the day phase
Moon is rendered as a full circle instead of a crescent
Ground silhouette changes color with the sky instead of remaining dark
Loop transition from end back to start causes a visible glitch or color pop
Sun rays do not rotate or look static
